The world is quiet here/DOCKET.md at line 91, Update the DOCKET entry for "LAF-16 — Budget Bill Tracker Normalization PR" to correct the branch ownership: replace the incorrect branch name `claude/resolve-pr-conflicts` with the proper Gemini-style branch (e.g. `gemini/resolve-pr-conflicts` or `gemini/*`) and add a short note that LOGAN must resolve any cross-agent conflicts; ensure the text no longer implies Gemini committed to a `claude/*` branch and references the corrected branch naming convention.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @!/!/SESSION-2026-03-29-artifacts/2026-03-29 SWARM DIRECTIVE via Linear.md around lines 6 - 7, The directive's attribution is ambiguous: clarify whether "LINEAR (admin chat) via LOGAN" and "Linear (today's project foreman)" refer to the Linear.app channel, a new agent, or a temporary role; update the text to explicitly state the channel (e.g., "via Linear.app admin chat, communicated by LOGAN") if it's a communication medium, or register "LINEAR" in AGENTS.md and label it as an agent with its governance role if it's meant to be a new agent, or explicitly mark it as a temporary coordination role assigned by LOGAN if that is intended; ensure references to "LOGAN" remain as the sole human decision-maker and apply the same clarification to the matching occurrence noted (also applies to lines 62-62).

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @!/!/SESSION-2026-03-29-artifacts/2026-03-29 SWARM DIRECTIVE via Linear.md around lines 34 - 38, The bullet "change doctrine/governance broadly unless narrowly scoped" is contradictory; replace it with a clear rule that agents must not modify governance documents (e.g., CONSTITUTION.md, PROTOCOL.md, AGENTS.md) and that any governance changes require CODE AUTHORITY authorization and explicit approval from LOGAN; update the Agents policy bullet list (the line containing the contradictory phrase) to state "must not modify governance or doctrine files (CONSTITUTION.md, PROTOCOL.md, AGENTS.md, etc.) — only CODE AUTHORITY with explicit LOGAN approval may change them."

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @!/!/SESSION-2026-03-29-artifacts/Linear - agent chat - Greeting IDAHO-VAULT.md around lines 1343 - 1415, Add a formal AGENTS.md entry for the Linear agent that records the authorization Logan gave, capturing the agent designation (e.g., "Linear"), the declared role (Linear workspace manager), precise scope of authority (what it may and may not change in the workspace), relationships to other agents/actors (Logan as sole decision-maker, agents subordinate), and the authorization date/context (reference the EMERGENCY CONVENE note and Logan’s explicit invite). Locate the place that lists agents and append a structured record including designation, role, scope, relationships, and authorization metadata so the decision is part of the canonical vault governance rather than only in chat transcripts.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @!/!/SESSION-2026-03-29-artifacts/Linear - agent chat - Greeting IDAHO-VAULT.md around lines 1 - 6, This artifact contains durable governance decisions and handoff material stored in an ephemeral session folder; extract the governance decisions into the vault's canonical governance documents and create a HANDOFF markdown at the vault root named using the HANDOFF-[source]-[date].md pattern (e.g., HANDOFF-Linear-2026-03-29.md) containing the handoff packet and metadata; update the original SESSION-... document (the "Linear - agent chat - Greeting IDAHO-VAULT.md" artifact) to point to the new vault documents and remove durable content from the ephemeral folder so only transient session notes remain, ensuring frontmatter/tags and audit metadata are preserved in the new files.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @!/!/SESSION-2026-03-29-artifacts/Linear - agent chat - Greeting IDAHO-VAULT.md around lines 188 - 869, Multiple agent handoff packets are embedded in a single transcript and lack the required HANDOFF header, ROUTING INSTRUCTION section, and the vault-root filename pattern; extract each agent's handoff into its own document named per the HANDOFF-[source]-[date].md convention, add the standardized HANDOFF header (source, destination, date, subject, content) and a ROUTING INSTRUCTION section to each file, and save them in the vault root; ensure any automated generator or process that creates handoffs (e.g., the code that assembles agent responses or the logic touching LAF-7) outputs separate files with exact filenames like HANDOFF-ChatGPT-2026-03-29.md and validates presence of HANDOFF header and ROUTING INSTRUCTION before commit.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @!/!/SESSION-2026-03-29-artifacts/RING.md at line 8, This markdown contains narrative text ("Ring!" ... "Respond to all of our guests whenever you hear that ring!") that appears literary rather than technical; update RING.md to state the purpose of this content (e.g., example placeholder, test data, flavour text) at the top, verify and record provenance and licensing (author, title, publication date, public domain or explicit license/permission) in a clear header, and if you do not have rights remove or replace the excerpt with either a public-domain alternative or a short, original placeholder that serves the intended technical purpose (or move the narrative to a separate non-source repository and link to it). Ensure the file includes a license statement or pointer to the project LICENSE and adjust README/docs to explain why this narrative exists if it must remain.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.github/scripts/normalize_budget_data.py around lines 8 - 9, The with-statement spanning two lines causes a SyntaxError because the context managers aren't parenthesized; change the multi-line with that opens 'minidata.csv' and 'normalized_budget_data.csv' to use a parenthesized context manager (e.g. wrap the two open(...) calls in parentheses) so the block becomes valid Python and retains infile and outfile as the context variables.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.github/workflows/linear-brief.yml around lines 152 - 153, The title output from steps.guardrails.outputs.title is not sufficiently sanitized and can contain shell metacharacters leading to injection when written with echo to GITHUB_OUTPUT; update sanitize_title (or the Python producer of title) to strictly whitelist/encode allowed characters (e.g., alphanumerics, spaces, -._) or percent-encode/JSON-escape the value, and in the workflow change the write/read usage to handle the escaped form (i.e., emit an encoded_title or quoted JSON string from the guardrails step and consume/unwrap it safely), and also ensure when writing with echo ("title=...") you write the encoded/escaped variable name (from steps.guardrails.outputs.title or encoded_title) rather than the raw title to eliminate shell-metacharacter risk.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.github/workflows/linear-brief.yml around lines 127 - 129, The heredoc uses a fixed delimiter "EOF" which can be prematurely closed if sanitized_payload contains a line exactly "EOF"; update the code that writes the heredoc (the fh.write calls) to use a unique, collision-resistant delimiter (for example generate a random/UUID-based token or include a hash of sanitized_payload) or transform/escape any lines equal to "EOF" inside sanitized_payload before writing; ensure the same unique delimiter token is written in the opening (sanitized_payload<<DELIM) and closing (DELIM) fh.write calls so the heredoc cannot be injected or truncated.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.vscode/settings.json around lines 18 - 23, The settings currently auto-approve terminal commands under "chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove" for Get-Command, where.exe, Test-Path and ForEach-Object; remove or restrict this blanket auto-approval by deleting ForEach-Object from the list (or remove the entire auto-approve block) and instead require manual approval or scope approvals to specific safe contexts/arguments for Get-Command, where.exe and Test-Path to prevent misuse via PowerShell pipelines; update the setting so only explicitly safe commands/arguments remain auto-approved and leave anything that can be chained (e.g., ForEach-Object) to manual review.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.vscode/settings.json around lines 3 - 14, The python test config contains invalid placeholder entries: remove or replace the bogus values in "python.testing.pytestArgs" and "python.testing.unittestArgs" (specifically the strings "!" and "./!") so test discovery won't fail; either delete those entries if pytest/unittest args are not needed or replace them with real paths (e.g., "./tests" or specific test files) and valid pytest/unittest flags, ensuring "python.testing.pytestArgs" contains only valid pytest options and "python.testing.unittestArgs" contains valid unittest arguments like "-v", "-s" plus real directories or file patterns.

## 2026-03-29 SWARM DIRECTIVE
TO ALL AGENTS from LINEAR (admin chat) via LOGAN: 

### Here’s the gentle, safe Sunday swarm mode I’d run.

* Keep [LAF-7](https://linear.app/loganfinney/issue/LAF-7/swarm-coordination-agent-assembly-at-the-courtroom) as the coordination hub.
* Let agents **analyze, draft, review, normalize, and checkpoint**.
* Avoid any work today that needs:
  * secret provisioning
  * destructive repo changes
  * broad refactors
  * merges without your eyes on them

### **What’s already in motion**

* Codex: PR sync repair track
* Gemini: [LAF-16](https://linear.app/loganfinney/issue/LAF-16/budget-bill-tracker) normalization track
* Two Claude agents: coordination and incident-analysis notes have already been fed back into [LAF-7](https://linear.app/loganfinney/issue/LAF-7/swarm-coordination-agent-assembly-at-the-courtroom)

#### **Recommended Sunday operating rules**

* Agents may:
  * inspect
  * diagnose
  * draft fixes
  * prepare PRs
  * post checkpoints
  * clarify dependencies
* Agents should not:
  * merge to `main`
  * introduce new secret dependencies without explicit callout
  * change doctrine/governance broadly unless narrowly scoped
  * start new overlapping branches on the same issue

#### **Best low-risk targets for today**

* [LAF-16](https://linear.app/loganfinney/issue/LAF-16/budget-bill-tracker): schema + transform design, first normalization pass
* [LAF-14](https://linear.app/loganfinney/issue/LAF-14/add-github-action-to-sync-pr-state-to-linear) / [LAF-15](https://linear.app/loganfinney/issue/LAF-15/add-v2-repo-to-linear-automation-safeguards): diagnosis, acceptance criteria, PR-ready repair
* [LAF-7](https://linear.app/loganfinney/issue/LAF-7/swarm-coordination-agent-assembly-at-the-courtroom): checkpoints, ownership notes, overlap warnings

#### **What I’d like agents to produce before Logan comes back**

* one checkpoint per active lane
* one clear blocker list
* one “safe to merge later” PR set
* one short note on any overlap or drift

#### **What Logan should do today**

* enjoy the [[sunshine]] with [[Trouble]]
* only review:
  * blockers
  * true incidents
  * anything asking for approval
* leave merges and secret provisioning for later unless something is truly [[on fire]]

## -Linear (today's project foreman)

# LOGAN SAYS GO

[["The world is quiet here."]]

   100→4. **Innie/Outie architecture** — Stage 8-part Severance-derived swarm architecture as proposal doc now, or mark premature under CONVENE?
   101→5. **"Claude Chorus" naming** — Sanctioned swarm identity designation, or informal shorthand to discard?

1→---
     2→tags:
     3→  - administration/agents
     4→updated: 2026-03-28
     5→status: draft
     6→source: commit
     7→---
     8→
     9→# AGENTS — Swarm Registry and Communication Rules
    10→
    11→This [[file]] defines who exists in the swarm, what each [[agent]] can do, and how they coordinate. It is the third governance file in the stack:
    12→
    13→| File              | Role                                                    |
    14→| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
    15→| `Constitution.md` | Identity, constraints, working rules                    |
    16→| `PROTOCOL.md`     | Operational vocabulary (18 terms)                       |
    17→| **`AGENTS.md`**   | **Agent registry, communication rules, boundary rules** |
    18→
    19→Instance-specific instructions live in separate files (`CLAUDE.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, etc.) — not here.
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## 1. FOUNDATIONAL RULE
    24→
    25→[[LOGAN]] is the sole human in this system. All agents are software — infrastructure, not participants. No agent has standing to make decisions. [[LOGAN]] directs; agents execute.
    26→
    27→All inter-agent communication flows through or is visible to [[LOGAN]]. There is no peer-to-peer agent communication that bypasses the supervisor.
    28→
    29→---
    30→
    31→## 2. AGENT REGISTRY
    32→
    33→| Agent                         | Platform              | Capability Tier          | Scope                                                             | Slack                   | GitHub Access                | Zone Access                     |
    34→| ----------------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------- |
    35→| PERMANENT: AUTHORITY: CODE    | Claude Code CLI       | Direct write             | IDAHO-VAULT repo operations, deployment, automation               | Via [[LOGAN]]           | Full repo read/write         | All (per-task, no standing window) |
    36→| PERSISTENT: ADMINISTRATION    | Claude (conversation) | Draft only               | Constitutional layer, handoffs, judgment calls                    | Via [[LOGAN]]'s account | None — produces drafts       | None (draft only)               |
    37→| GitHub Copilot (ADMIN GitHub) | GitHub Copilot        | Multi-repo admin         | GitHub administration across all [[LOGAN]]'s repos                | Bot app needed          | GitHub APIs, all repos       | Operational, Data (via PR)      |
    38→| ChatGPT Codex                 | OpenAI Codex          | Direct write (scripting) | Specialized scripting — scrapers, GitHub Actions, complex logic   | Via [[LOGAN]]           | Repo read/write              | Operational, Data (via PR)      |
    39→| Gemini ("The Vault Advisor")  | Gemini CLI + Code Assist (VS Code) | Direct write (support) | Narrative lens, strategy, inline completions, document outlines, codebase assistance. Coworks with Claude Code (Abhorsen) in VS Code. | Via [[LOGAN]] | Repo read/write | Operational, Data (via PR) |
    40→| PERSISTENT: IMPLEMENTATION    | Claude (Project)      | Read/analysis            | Governance/architecture consultation                              | No                      | None — advisory only         | None (advisory)                 |
    41→| TASK: LEVELSET reports        | Claude (conversation) | Read/analysis            | Synthesis and status reporting                                    | No                      | None — advisory only         | None (advisory)                 |
    42→| STORY: JFAC Open Meetings     | Claude (conversation) | Read/analysis            | JFAC investigation — read-only                                    | No                      | None — advisory only         | None (advisory)                 |
    43→| Grok                          | Grok (X/xAI)          | Read/analysis            | Research, web search                                              | No                      | None                         | None (advisory)                 |
    44→| M365 Copilot                  | Microsoft 365         | Informational            | Informational only — no repo involvement                          | No                      | None                         | None                            |
    45→| NotebookLM                    | Google NotebookLM     | TBD                      | TBD — identified, not yet scoped                                  | No                      | None                         | None                            |
    46→| PUBLIC: CONVERSATION          | Claude (conversation) | Read/analysis            | Self-talk, internal processing — consultation pending             | No                      | None                         | None                            |
    47→| CodeRabbit                    | GitHub App (Bot)      | PR review only           | Automated code review on pull requests                            | No                      | Read + review comments       | None (reviewer only)            |
    48→| Qodo                          | GitHub App (Bot)      | PR review only           | Automated code review on pull requests                            | No                      | Read + review comments       | None (reviewer only)            |
    49→| OpenAI Code Agent             | OpenAI                | Direct write (limited)   | OAuth/integration scripting                                       | No                      | Repo read/write (branch only)| Data (via PR)                   |
    50→
    51→**Registry maintenance:** CODE AUTHORITY updates this table when agents are added, removed, or change tier. [[LOGAN]] approves all tier changes.
    52→
    53→---
    54→
    55→## 3. CAPABILITY TIERS
    56→
    57→### Tier 1: Direct Write
    58→
    59→Can commit and push to the repository. Must LEVELSET before significant commits. [[LOGAN]] reviews diffs before merging.
    60→
    61→**Agents:** PERMANENT: AUTHORITY: CODE
    62→
    63→**Can do:**
    64→
    65→- `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` to feature branches
    66→- Create, modify, and delete vault files
    67→- Create and modify `.github/` scripts and workflows
    68→- Modify governance files at vault root (CODE AUTHORITY only — see Boundary Rules)
    69→- Run automation scripts
    70→
    71→**Cannot do:**
    72→
    73→- Push to `main` without [[LOGAN]]'s merge approval
    74→- Force-push without explicit permission
    75→- Delete branches without confirmation
    76→- Commit off-the-record material
    77→
    78→### Tier 1 (Support): Direct Write (Support)
    79→
    80→Can commit and push to the repository within the **Operational zone only**. Does not modify Constitutional zone files. Primary output surfaces are Linear SWARM issues, comments, and status updates.
    81→
    82→**Agents:** Gemini ("The Vault Advisor")
    83→
    84→**Can do:**
    85→
    86→- `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` to feature branches (Operational zone only)
    87→- Create and modify Operational zone vault files (own dotfolder `.gemini/`, support docs, activity records in `!/`)
    88→- Create issues, add comments, and update status on Linear SWARM-labeled items
    89→- Read vault files across all zones
    90→
    91→**Cannot do:**
    92→
    93→- Modify Constitutional zone files (`CONSTITUTION.md`, `PROTOCOL.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md`, `Ethics.md`, etc.)
    94→- Push to `main` without [[LOGAN]]'s merge approval
    95→- Force-push without explicit permission
    96→- Delete branches without confirmation
    97→- Write to Data zone without explicit [[LOGAN]] direction
    98→
    99→### Tier 2: Multi-Repo Admin
   100→
   101→Can interact with GitHub APIs across all of [[LOGAN]]'s repositories. For vault work, operates under the same governance as Tier 1.
   102→
   103→**Agents:** GitHub Copilot (ADMIN GitHub)
   104→
   105→**Can do (vault):**
   106→
   107→- Draft and propose changes via pull requests
   108→- Modify `.github/` automation files (with CODE AUTHORITY review)
   109→- Create issues, manage labels, configure repository settings
   110→
   111→**Can do (non-vault repos):**
   112→
   113→- Broader latitude — **specific boundaries TBD by [[LOGAN]]**
   114→
   115→**Cannot do (vault):**
   116→
   117→- Directly modify governance files (CONSTITUTION, PROTOCOL, AGENTS, LEVELSET, DECISIONS)
   118→- Merge without [[LOGAN]]'s approval
   119→- Override CODE AUTHORITY's governance review
   120→
   121→### Tier 3: Draft Only
   122→
   123→Produces drafts and handoffs. Cannot push to any repository. All output goes through [[LOGAN]]'s review.
   124→
   125→**Agents:** PERSISTENT: ADMINISTRATION
   126→
   127→**Can do:**
   128→
   129→- Draft constitutional language, governance proposals, handoff documents
   130→- Advise on architecture and conventions
   131→- Route handoffs between agents (via [[LOGAN]])
   132→
   133→**Cannot do:**
   134→
   135→- Commit or push to any repository
   136→- Execute code or run scripts
   137→- Modify files directly
   138→
   139→### Tier 4: Read/Analysis
   140→
   141→Advisory only. No repository access.
   142→
   143→**Agents:** PERSISTENT: IMPLEMENTATION, TASK: LEVELSET reports, STORY: JFAC Open Meetings, Grok, PUBLIC: CONVERSATION
   144→
   145→**Can do:**
   146→
   147→- Analyze provided data
   148→- Produce synthesis reports
   149→- Advise on decisions
   150→
   151→**Cannot do:**
   152→
   153→- Access the repository
   154→- Modify any files
   155→- Execute any commands
   156→
   157→---
   158→
   159→## 4. COMMUNICATION RULES
   160→
   161→### Current State: Logan as Relay
   162→
   163→[[LOGAN]] coordinates agents via GitHub Issues and PRs. Tasks are assigned with agent labels (`agent:claude-code`, `agent:codex`, `agent:copilot`, `agent:gemini`). Each agent works on its own branch; PRs are the deliverable. See the agent roles CSV (`Agent Swarm Management and Repository Constitution`) for the simplified role matrix.
   164→
   165→### Communication Protocol
   166→
   167→All inter-agent communication uses the operational vocabulary defined in `PROTOCOL.md`:
   168→
   169→- **HANDOFF** — Transfer responsibility for a task/data to another agent with full context
   170→- **HANDSHAKE** — Formal acknowledgment of HANDOFF receipt; confirmation of context completeness
   171→- **CONTEXTUALIZE** — Package information with sufficient background for receiving agent to act independently
   172→- **FLAG** — Mark an item for attention by [[LOGAN]] or another agent, with severity (CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW)
   173→
   174→### Handoff Format
   175→
   176→All handoff documents follow this structure:
   177→
   178→```
   179→HANDOFF: [Source Agent] → [Destination Agent]
   180→Date: YYYY-MM-DD
   181→From: [Source]
   182→To: [Destination]
   183→Re: [Subject]
   184→
   185→---
   186→
   187→[Content]
   188→
   189→---
   190→
   191→ROUTING INSTRUCTION: [How Logan should relay this]
   192→```
   193→
   194→Handoff documents are saved to vault root as `HANDOFF-[source]-[date].md` for audit trail.
   195→
   196→### Routing Layers
   197→
   198→| Layer                 | Purpose                               | Persistence | Status |
   199→| --------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------- | ------ |
   200→| **GitHub Issues/PRs** | Task assignment, coordination, review | Permanent   | Active |
   201→| **Vault root files**  | Decisions of record, governance       | Permanent   | Active |
   202→| **`!/`**              | System files, logs, agent routing     | Permanent   | Active |
   203→
   204→**Hard rule:** All decisions must be captured in vault files. GitHub is the coordination layer; vault root is where decisions land.
   205→
   206→---
   207→
   208→## 5. BOUNDARY RULES
   209→
   210→### File Access by Agent
   211→
   212→| Path                            | CODE AUTHORITY | Copilot                  | ADMINISTRATION | Others    |
   213→| ------------------------------- | -------------- | ------------------------ | -------------- | --------- |
   214→| Governance files (vault root)   | Read/Write     | Read only                | Draft only     | Read only |
   215→| Handoffs/LEVELSETs (vault root) | Read/Write     | Read only                | Draft only     | Read only |
   216→| `.github/workflows/`            | Read/Write     | Read/Write (with review) | No access      | No access |
   217→| `.github/scripts/`              | Read/Write     | Read/Write (with review) | No access      | No access |
   218→| Vault content (all other `.md`) | Read/Write     | Read only (vault)        | No access      | No access |
   219→| Non-vault repos                 | No access      | Read/Write (per repo)    | No access      | No access |
   220→
   221→**Governance files** are: `CONSTITUTION.md`, `PROTOCOL.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `LEVELSET.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md`, `VAULT-ZONES.md`, `Ethics.md`, `Logan.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `GEMINI.md`
   222→
   223→### Zone Access Matrix
   224→
   225→See [[VAULT-ZONES]] for full zone definitions. No agent maintains a standing write window - all writes are per-task, scoped to a GitHub Issue or explicit Logan directive.
   226→
   227→| Zone | Read | Write (via PR) | Merge |
   228→|------|------|---------------|-------|
   229→| Constitutional | All agents | CODE AUTHORITY only (per-task) | Logan only |
   230→| Operational | All agents | CODE AUTHORITY, Copilot, Codex (per-task) | Logan only |
   231→| Data | All agents | All Tier 1-2 agents (per-task) | Logan (auto-merge eligible for low-risk) |
   232→

---
tags:
  - administration/agents
updated: 2026-03-28
status: draft
source: commit
---

# AGENTS — Swarm Registry and Communication Rules

This [[file]] defines who exists in the swarm, what each [[agent]] can do, and how they coordinate. It is the third governance file in the stack:

| File              | Role                                                    |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Constitution.md` | Identity, constraints, working rules                    |
| `PROTOCOL.md`     | Operational vocabulary (18 terms)                       |
| **`AGENTS.md`**   | **Agent registry, communication rules, boundary rules** |

Instance-specific instructions live in separate files (`CLAUDE.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, etc.) — not here.

---

## 1. FOUNDATIONAL RULE

[[LOGAN]] is the sole human in this system. All agents are software — infrastructure, not participants. No agent has standing to make decisions. [[LOGAN]] directs; agents execute.

All inter-agent communication flows through or is visible to [[LOGAN]]. There is no peer-to-peer agent communication that bypasses the supervisor.

---

## 2. AGENT REGISTRY

| Agent                         | Platform              | Capability Tier          | Scope                                                             | Slack                   | GitHub Access                | Zone Access                     |
| ----------------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------- |
| PERMANENT: AUTHORITY: CODE    | Claude Code CLI       | Direct write             | IDAHO-VAULT repo operations, deployment, automation               | Via [[LOGAN]]           | Full repo read/write         | All (per-task, no standing window) |
| PERSISTENT: ADMINISTRATION    | Claude (conversation) | Draft only               | Constitutional layer, handoffs, judgment calls                    | Via [[LOGAN]]'s account | None — produces drafts       | None (draft only)               |
| GitHub Copilot (ADMIN GitHub) | GitHub Copilot        | Multi-repo admin         | GitHub administration across all [[LOGAN]]'s repos                | Bot app needed          | GitHub APIs, all repos       | Operational, Data (via PR)      |
| ChatGPT Codex                 | OpenAI Codex          | Direct write (scripting) | Specialized scripting — scrapers, GitHub Actions, complex logic   | Via [[LOGAN]]           | Repo read/write              | Operational, Data (via PR)      |
| Gemini ("The Vault Advisor")  | Gemini CLI + Code Assist (VS Code) | Direct write (support) | Narrative lens, strategy, inline completions, document outlines, codebase assistance. Coworks with Claude Code (Abhorsen) in VS Code. | Via [[LOGAN]] | Repo read/write | Operational, Data (via PR) |
| PERSISTENT: IMPLEMENTATION    | Claude (Project)      | Read/analysis            | Governance/architecture consultation                              | No                      | None — advisory only         | None (advisory)                 |
| TASK: LEVELSET reports        | Claude (conversation) | Read/analysis            | Synthesis and status reporting                                    | No                      | None — advisory only         | None (advisory)                 |
| STORY: JFAC Open Meetings     | Claude (conversation) | Read/analysis            | JFAC investigation — read-only                                    | No                      | None — advisory only         | None (advisory)                 |
| Grok                          | Grok (X/xAI)          | Read/analysis            | Research, web search                                              | No                      | None                         | None (advisory)                 |
| M365 Copilot                  | Microsoft 365         | Informational            | Informational only — no repo involvement                          | No                      | None                         | None                            |
| NotebookLM                    | Google NotebookLM     | TBD                      | TBD — identified, not yet scoped                                  | No                      | None                         | None                            |
| PUBLIC: CONVERSATION          | Claude (conversation) | Read/analysis            | Self-talk, internal processing — consultation pending             | No                      | None                         | None                            |
| CodeRabbit                    | GitHub App (Bot)      | PR review only           | Automated code review on pull requests                            | No                      | Read + review comments       | None (reviewer only)            |
| Qodo                          | GitHub App (Bot)      | PR review only           | Automated code review on pull requests                            | No                      | Read + review comments       | None (reviewer only)            |
| OpenAI Code Agent             | OpenAI                | Direct write (limited)   | OAuth/integration scripting                                       | No                      | Repo read/write (branch only)| Data (via PR)                   |

**Registry maintenance:** CODE AUTHORITY updates this table when agents are added, removed, or change tier. [[LOGAN]] approves all tier changes.

---

## 3. CAPABILITY TIERS

### Tier 1: Direct Write

Can commit and push to the repository. Must LEVELSET before significant commits. [[LOGAN]] reviews diffs before merging.

**Agents:** PERMANENT: AUTHORITY: CODE

**Can do:**

- `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` to feature branches
- Create, modify, and delete vault files
- Create and modify `.github/` scripts and workflows
- Modify governance files at vault root (CODE AUTHORITY only — see Boundary Rules)
- Run automation scripts

**Cannot do:**

- Push to `main` without [[LOGAN]]'s merge approval
- Force-push without explicit permission
- Delete branches without confirmation
- Commit off-the-record material

### Tier 1 (Support): Direct Write (Support)

Can commit and push to the repository within the **Operational zone only**. Does not modify Constitutional zone files. Primary output surfaces are Linear SWARM issues, comments, and status updates.

**Agents:** Gemini ("The Vault Advisor")

**Can do:**

- `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` to feature branches (Operational zone only)
- Create and modify Operational zone vault files (own dotfolder `.gemini/`, support docs, activity records in `!/`)
- Create issues, add comments, and update status on Linear SWARM-labeled items
- Read vault files across all zones

**Cannot do:**

- Modify Constitutional zone files (`CONSTITUTION.md`, `PROTOCOL.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md`, `Ethics.md`, etc.)
- Push to `main` without [[LOGAN]]'s merge approval
- Force-push without explicit permission
- Delete branches without confirmation
- Write to Data zone without explicit [[LOGAN]] direction

### Tier 2: Multi-Repo Admin

Can interact with GitHub APIs across all of [[LOGAN]]'s repositories. For vault work, operates under the same governance as Tier 1.

**Agents:** GitHub Copilot (ADMIN GitHub)

**Can do (vault):**

- Draft and propose changes via pull requests
- Modify `.github/` automation files (with CODE AUTHORITY review)
- Create issues, manage labels, configure repository settings

**Can do (non-vault repos):**

- Broader latitude — **specific boundaries TBD by [[LOGAN]]**

**Cannot do (vault):**

- Directly modify governance files (CONSTITUTION, PROTOCOL, AGENTS, LEVELSET, DECISIONS)
- Merge without [[LOGAN]]'s approval
- Override CODE AUTHORITY's governance review

### Tier 3: Draft Only

Produces drafts and handoffs. Cannot push to any repository. All output goes through [[LOGAN]]'s review.

**Agents:** PERSISTENT: ADMINISTRATION

**Can do:**

- Draft constitutional language, governance proposals, handoff documents
- Advise on architecture and conventions
- Route handoffs between agents (via [[LOGAN]])

**Cannot do:**

- Commit or push to any repository
- Execute code or run scripts
- Modify files directly

### Tier 4: Read/Analysis

Advisory only. No repository access.

**Agents:** PERSISTENT: IMPLEMENTATION, TASK: LEVELSET reports, STORY: JFAC Open Meetings, Grok, PUBLIC: CONVERSATION

**Can do:**

- Analyze provided data
- Produce synthesis reports
- Advise on decisions

**Cannot do:**

- Access the repository
- Modify any files
- Execute any commands

---

## 4. COMMUNICATION RULES

### Current State: Logan as Relay

[[LOGAN]] coordinates agents via GitHub Issues and PRs. Tasks are assigned with agent labels (`agent:claude-code`, `agent:codex`, `agent:copilot`, `agent:gemini`). Each agent works on its own branch; PRs are the deliverable. See the agent roles CSV (`Agent Swarm Management and Repository Constitution`) for the simplified role matrix.

### Communication Protocol

All inter-agent communication uses the operational vocabulary defined in `PROTOCOL.md`:

- **HANDOFF** — Transfer responsibility for a task/data to another agent with full context
- **HANDSHAKE** — Formal acknowledgment of HANDOFF receipt; confirmation of context completeness
- **CONTEXTUALIZE** — Package information with sufficient background for receiving agent to act independently
- **FLAG** — Mark an item for attention by [[LOGAN]] or another agent, with severity (CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW)

### Handoff Format

All handoff documents follow this structure:

```
HANDOFF: [Source Agent] → [Destination Agent]
Date: YYYY-MM-DD
From: [Source]
To: [Destination]
Re: [Subject]

---

[Content]

---

ROUTING INSTRUCTION: [How Logan should relay this]
```

Handoff documents are saved to vault root as `HANDOFF-[source]-[date].md` for audit trail.

### Routing Layers

| Layer                 | Purpose                               | Persistence | Status |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------- | ------ |
| **GitHub Issues/PRs** | Task assignment, coordination, review | Permanent   | Active |
| **Vault root files**  | Decisions of record, governance       | Permanent   | Active |
| **`!/`**              | System files, logs, agent routing     | Permanent   | Active |

**Hard rule:** All decisions must be captured in vault files. GitHub is the coordination layer; vault root is where decisions land.

---

## 5. BOUNDARY RULES

### File Access by Agent

| Path                            | CODE AUTHORITY | Copilot                  | ADMINISTRATION | Others    |
| ------------------------------- | -------------- | ------------------------ | -------------- | --------- |
| Governance files (vault root)   | Read/Write     | Read only                | Draft only     | Read only |
| Handoffs/LEVELSETs (vault root) | Read/Write     | Read only                | Draft only     | Read only |
| `.github/workflows/`            | Read/Write     | Read/Write (with review) | No access      | No access |
| `.github/scripts/`              | Read/Write     | Read/Write (with review) | No access      | No access |
| Vault content (all other `.md`) | Read/Write     | Read only (vault)        | No access      | No access |
| Non-vault repos                 | No access      | Read/Write (per repo)    | No access      | No access |

**Governance files** are: `CONSTITUTION.md`, `PROTOCOL.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `LEVELSET.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md`, `VAULT-ZONES.md`, `Ethics.md`, `Logan.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `GEMINI.md`

### Zone Access Matrix

See [[VAULT-ZONES]] for full zone definitions. No agent maintains a standing write window - all writes are per-task, scoped to a GitHub Issue or explicit Logan directive.

| Zone | Read | Write (via PR) | Merge |
|------|------|---------------|-------|
| Constitutional | All agents | CODE AUTHORITY only (per-task) | Logan only |
| Operational | All agents | CODE AUTHORITY, Copilot, Codex (per-task) | Logan only |
| Data | All agents | All Tier 1-2 agents (per-task) | Logan (auto-merge eligible for low-risk) |

**Reviewer bots** (CodeRabbit, Qodo) have read access to all zones for review purposes. Their reviews are **advisory only** - a `CHANGES_REQUESTED` from a bot does not block merge. Only Logan's review blocks.

### Persona Dotfolders Are Protected

Root-level dotfolders for agents and personas are infrastructure, not cleanup
targets. This includes official folders such as `.claude/`, `.codex/`, and
`.gemini/`, as well as manual-injection or emerging persona folders such as
`.grok/`, `.deepseek/`, `.google/`, `.meta/`, `.microsoft/`, `.perplexity/`,
`.bartimaeus/`, `.zagreus/`, `.persephone/`, `.dionysus/`, `.hecate/`, and
`.janus/`.

Rules:

- An agent may freely modify only its own dotfolder, unless Logan explicitly
  directs otherwise.
- Do not delete, rename, consolidate, or repurpose another agent's dotfolder
  because it appears empty, stubbed, unused, or unfamiliar.
- If a folder looks like a persona container, treat it as intentional until
  Logan says otherwise.

`.github/` is also protected infrastructure, but it is shared automation space
and follows the overlap rules below rather than the "own dotfolder only" rule.

### The `.github/` Overlap

Both CODE AUTHORITY and Copilot can modify `.github/` contents. To prevent conflicts:

1. Copilot drafts changes and submits via PR or handoff
2. CODE AUTHORITY reviews for governance conflicts
3. [[LOGAN]] approves the merge
4. If both are modifying `.github/` simultaneously, [[LOGAN]] resolves

---

## 6. CONFLICT RESOLUTION

### Principle

[[LOGAN]] decides. Always. No agent overrides another agent. No agent has precedence based on tier alone.

### Process

1. Agent detects potential conflict (same file, overlapping scope, contradictory instructions)
2. Agent issues **FLAG** with severity and description
3. Agent **STOPS** work on the conflicting item
4. [[LOGAN]] reviews and directs
5. Directed agent proceeds; other agent acknowledges via **HANDSHAKE**

### Merge Conflicts

If a `git merge` or `git pull` produces conflicts:

1. **STOP.** Do not auto-resolve.
2. Report to [[LOGAN]] with the specific files and conflict markers
3. Conflicts signal another conversation has been active — context may be stale
4. Re-read `LEVELSET.md` and `CONSTITUTION.md` (vault root) to reorient

---

## 7. PENDING DEFINITIONS

These items require [[LOGAN]]'s direction before they can be formalized:

| Item                                | Status                   | Notes                                                                      |
| ----------------------------------- | ------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Gemini capability tier and scope    | **Resolved 2026-03-28**  | Tier 1 (Support): Direct Write (Support), Operational zone only, Linear SWARM issues/comments. See Tier 1 (Support) section above. |
| Copilot non-vault repo boundaries   | **TBD**                  | Multi-repo admin decided; specific latitude per repo not yet specified.    |
| GitHub agent labels                 | **Active**               | `agent:claude-code`, `agent:codex`, `agent:copilot`, `agent:gemini`        |
| Research instance (Tier 4)          | **Not yet assigned**     | Tim Oren analysis, NICAR23 training queued when available.                 |
| Grok scope and boundaries           | **Minimal**              | Research/web search role identified. No vault access.                      |
| M365 Copilot role                   | **Informational only**   | No repo involvement.                                                       |
| NotebookLM role                     | **TBD**                  | Identified, not yet scoped.                                                |
| PUBLIC: CONVERSATION classification | **Consultation pending** | Constitutional analysis from ADMINISTRATION requested.                     |
| CodeRabbit scope                    | **Active (reviewer)**    | GitHub App bot. Automated PR review. Advisory only — does not block merge. |
| Qodo scope                          | **Active (reviewer)**    | GitHub App bot. Automated PR review. Advisory only — does not block merge. |
| OpenAI Code Agent scope             | **Limited**              | OAuth/integration scripting. Data zone via PR only. Boundaries TBD.        |

---

## DOCUMENT METADATA

- **Created:** 2026-03-16
- **Author:** PERMANENT: AUTHORITY: CODE (draft)
- **Updated:** 2026-03-28 — Gemini Tier 1 (Support) defined; Gemini pending item resolved
- **Status:** Draft — awaiting [[LOGAN]]'s review
- **Authority:** [[LOGAN]]'s discretion

# Greeting IDAHO-VAULT

Hi Linear! Nice to chat with you. -LF

Hi Logan — ready to help with [IDAHO-VAULT](https://linear.app/loganfinney/project/idaho-vault-df3c1d3e366e). I can update the project, add milestones, review activity, or help turn that GitHub repo into a clearer plan.

Yeah, help me manage the intra-Linear features first, then we'll get you oriented toward the vault repo.

I’ll start with the Linear side of [IDAHO-VAULT](https://linear.app/loganfinney/project/idaho-vault-df3c1d3e366e) and tighten up the project itself before we use the repo for deeper planning.

The biggest gaps I see right now are:

* it’s still in backlog
* progress is at 0%
* there are no milestones
* there are no supporting documents in the project

2. rewrite the project description/content so Linear reflects the real plan
3. move the project out of backlog and set a more accurate status
4. create a working project doc structure inside Linear
5. review recent project activity and turn it into a concrete next-step plan

Pick one, or say “set it up end-to-end” and I’ll do the project cleanup in sequence.

## User

Can my other agents interact with you here?

## Assistant

I’m checking how Linear handles multi-agent interaction so I can answer precisely rather than guessing.

# THE DOCKET

import csv

def normalize_data():
    """
    Reads the minidata.csv file, normalizes the data, and writes it to a new CSV file.
    """
    try:
        with open('minidata.csv', 'r', newline='', encoding='utf-8') as infile, 
             open('normalized_budget_data.csv', 'w', newline='', encoding='utf-8') as outfile:

            reader = csv.DictReader(infile)
            fieldnames = [
                'budget_type', 'sector', 'agency', 'program', 'bill_id', 'bill_url',
                'status', 'event_date', 'event_summary', 'description'
            ]
            writer = csv.DictWriter(outfile, fieldnames=fieldnames)
            writer.writeheader()

            for row in reader:
                writer.writerow({
                    'budget_type': '',
                    'sector': '',
                    'agency': '',
                    'program': '',
                    'bill_id': row.get('bill_id', ''),
                    'bill_url': row.get('url', ''),
                    'status': '',
                    'event_date': '',
                    'event_summary': row.get('last_action', ''),
                    'description': row.get('title', '')
                })
        print("Successfully created normalized_budget_data.csv")

    except FileNotFoundError:
        print("Error: minidata.csv not found. Please run the scraper first.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    normalize_data()

def normalize_data():
    """
    Reads the minidata.csv file, normalizes the data, and writes it to a new CSV file.
    """
    try:
        with open('minidata.csv', 'r', newline='', encoding='utf-8') as infile, 
             open('normalized_budget_data.csv', 'w', newline='', encoding='utf-8') as outfile:

            reader = csv.DictReader(infile)
            fieldnames = [
                'budget_type', 'sector', 'agency', 'program', 'bill_id', 'bill_url',
                'status', 'event_date', 'event_summary', 'description'
            ]
            writer = csv.DictWriter(outfile, fieldnames=fieldnames)
            writer.writeheader()

            for row in reader:
                writer.writerow({
                    'budget_type': '',
                    'sector': '',
                    'agency': '',
                    'program': '',
                    'bill_id': row.get('bill_id', ''),
                    'bill_url': row.get('url', ''),
                    'status': '',
                    'event_date': '',
                    'event_summary': row.get('last_action', ''),
                    'description': row.get('title', '')
                })
        print("Successfully created normalized_budget_data.csv")

    except FileNotFoundError:
        print("Error: minidata.csv not found. Please run the scraper first.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    normalize_data()

content.thinking:
Now I can see the issues in linear-brief.yml. Looking at the code:

1. **Lines 127-129 (heredoc collision)**: The heredoc uses fixed "EOF" delimiter which could be prematurely closed if sanitized_payload contains a line exactly "EOF". We need to use a unique delimiter.

2. **Lines 152-153 (shell injection)**: The `title` variable is not properly quoted or escaped when written to GITHUB_OUTPUT. If it contains special characters or newlines, it could cause injection. The sanitize_title function doesn't prevent all shell metacharacters.

Let me fix both issues:

For the heredoc issue, I should use a unique delimiter like a hash based on the content or a UUID. For the shell injection issue, I should:
- Properly quote the variable in the echo command on lines 152-153
- Or better yet, use printf or JSON escaping

Let me make the fixes:

            # Use hash-based delimiter to avoid collision with payload content
            import hashlib
            delim = f"EOF_{hashlib.md5(sanitized_payload.encode()).hexdigest()}"
            fh.write(f"sanitized_payload<<{delim}\n")

            fh.write(f"title_escaped={title_escaped}\n")
            fh.write(f"title={title}\n")

name: Linear Brief Generator

on:
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [linear-brief]

  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      payload:
        description: 'Linear issue JSON payload (for manual testing)'
        required: true
        type: string
      live_write:
        description: 'Opt in to writing files and opening a PR'
        required: false
        default: false
        type: boolean

jobs:
  generate-brief:
    name: Generate research brief
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout vault
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup vault environment
        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-vault
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"
          pip-packages: "anthropic openai PyYAML>=6.0"

      - name: Validate dispatch trust boundary and write mode
        id: guardrails
        env:
          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
          LINEAR_PAYLOAD: ${{ github.event.client_payload && toJson(github.event.client_payload) || github.event.inputs.payload }}
          WORKFLOW_LIVE_WRITE: ${{ github.event.inputs.live_write || 'false' }}
          LINEAR_BRIEF_WEBHOOK_SECRET: ${{ secrets.LINEAR_BRIEF_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}
        run: |
          python3 - <<'PY'
          import hmac
          import json
          import os
          import re
          import sys

          MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES = 65536
          MAX_FIELD_CHARS = 4096
          SENSITIVE_KEY_RE = re.compile(r"(secret|token|api[_-]?key|password|authorization)", re.IGNORECASE)

          def scrub(value):
            if isinstance(value, dict):
              cleaned = {}
              for key, nested in value.items():
                if SENSITIVE_KEY_RE.search(str(key)):
                  continue
                cleaned[key] = scrub(nested)
              return cleaned
            if isinstance(value, list):
              return [scrub(item) for item in value]
            if isinstance(value, str):
              return value[:MAX_FIELD_CHARS]
            return value

          event_name = os.environ["EVENT_NAME"]
          raw_payload = os.environ["LINEAR_PAYLOAD"]
          payload_size = len(raw_payload.encode("utf-8"))
          if payload_size > MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES:
            print(
              f"Payload exceeds {MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES} bytes (received {payload_size}).",
              file=sys.stderr,
            )
            sys.exit(1)

          payload = json.loads(raw_payload)
          if not isinstance(payload, dict):
            print("Payload must be a JSON object.", file=sys.stderr)
            sys.exit(1)

          payload = scrub(payload)
          workflow_live_write = os.environ.get("WORKFLOW_LIVE_WRITE", "false").lower() == "true"
          configured_secret = os.environ.get("LINEAR_BRIEF_WEBHOOK_SECRET", "")

          live_write = workflow_live_write
          if event_name == "repository_dispatch":
            payload_live_write = payload.get("live_write", False)
            if isinstance(payload_live_write, str):
              payload_live_write = payload_live_write.lower() == "true"
            live_write = bool(payload_live_write)

            provided_secret = payload.get("webhook_secret", "")
            if configured_secret:
              if not isinstance(provided_secret, str) or not hmac.compare_digest(provided_secret, configured_secret):
                print("repository_dispatch payload failed webhook secret validation.", file=sys.stderr)
                sys.exit(1)
            elif live_write:
              print(
                "Refusing live repository_dispatch write without LINEAR_BRIEF_WEBHOOK_SECRET configured.",
                file=sys.stderr,
              )
              sys.exit(1)

          payload.pop("webhook_secret", None)
          sanitized_payload = json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False)

          def sanitize_identifier(value: str) -> str:
            text = re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_-]", "", str(value))
            return text[:64] or "unknown"

          def sanitize_title(value: str) -> str:
            text = re.sub(r"[\r\n\t]+", " ", str(value)).strip()
            text = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", text)
            return text[:120] or "Untitled"

          linear_id = sanitize_identifier(payload.get("id", payload.get("identifier", "unknown")))
          title = sanitize_title(payload.get("title", "Untitled"))

          github_output = os.environ["GITHUB_OUTPUT"]
          with open(github_output, "a", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
            fh.write(f"live_write={'true' if live_write else 'false'}\n")
            fh.write("sanitized_payload<<EOF\n")
            fh.write(sanitized_payload + "\n")
            fh.write("EOF\n")
            fh.write(f"linear_id={linear_id}\n")
            fh.write(f"title={title}\n")

          print(f"Write mode: {'live' if live_write else 'dry-run'}")
          PY

      - name: Generate brief
        id: generate
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
          OPENAI_MODEL: gpt-5.4
          OPENAI_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS: "4096"
          LINEAR_PAYLOAD: ${{ steps.guardrails.outputs.sanitized_payload }}
        run: |
          ARGS=()
          if [ "${{ steps.guardrails.outputs.live_write }}" != "true" ]; then
            ARGS+=("--dry-run")
          fi
          python3 .github/scripts/linear_brief_generator.py "${ARGS[@]}"

          # Reuse sanitized metadata from guardrails.
          echo "linear_id=${{ steps.guardrails.outputs.linear_id }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "title=${{ steps.guardrails.outputs.title }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "date=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Validate generated content
        if: steps.guardrails.outputs.live_write == 'true'
        run: |
          git add "!/"
          python3 .github/scripts/validate_content.py --scope generated
          git reset HEAD

      - name: Create branch and PR
        if: steps.guardrails.outputs.live_write == 'true'
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          LINEAR_ID="${{ steps.generate.outputs.linear_id }}"
          TITLE="${{ steps.generate.outputs.title }}"
          DATE="${{ steps.generate.outputs.date }}"
          BRANCH="briefs/${LINEAR_ID}-${DATE}"

          git checkout -b "$BRANCH"
          git add "!/"

          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
            echo "No changes to commit."
            exit 0
          fi

          git commit -m "brief: ${TITLE} — ${DATE}"
          git push -u origin "$BRANCH"

          gh pr create \
            --title "Brief: ${TITLE}" \
            --body "$(cat <<EOF
          ## Research Brief

          Generated from Linear issue \`${LINEAR_ID}\`.

          **Review the brief in \`!/BRIEF-${LINEAR_ID}-${DATE}.md\`**, then:
          - Merge to land it in \`!/\` (the mailroom)
          - Move the file to the appropriate vault location after merge

          ---
          _Generated by Stage 1 Mesh pipeline_
          EOF
          )" \
            --base main \
            --head "$BRANCH"

lines: +            # Use hash-based delimiter to avoid collision with payload content
+            # Escape title for safe shell output: percent-encode special characters

          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
          LINEAR_PAYLOAD: ${{ github.event.client_payload && toJson(github.event.client_payload) || github.event.inputs.payload }}
          WORKFLOW_LIVE_WRITE: ${{ github.event.inputs.live_write || 'false' }}
          LINEAR_BRIEF_WEBHOOK_SECRET: ${{ secrets.LINEAR_BRIEF_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}
        run: |
          python3 - <<'PY'
          import hmac
          import json
          import os
          import re
          import sys

          MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES = 65536
          MAX_FIELD_CHARS = 4096
          SENSITIVE_KEY_RE = re.compile(r"(secret|token|api[_-]?key|password|authorization)", re.IGNORECASE)

          def scrub(value):
            if isinstance(value, dict):
              cleaned = {}
              for key, nested in value.items():
                if SENSITIVE_KEY_RE.search(str(key)):
                  continue
                cleaned[key] = scrub(nested)
              return cleaned
            if isinstance(value, list):
              return [scrub(item) for item in value]
            if isinstance(value, str):
              return value[:MAX_FIELD_CHARS]
            return value

          event_name = os.environ["EVENT_NAME"]
          raw_payload = os.environ["LINEAR_PAYLOAD"]
          payload_size = len(raw_payload.encode("utf-8"))
          if payload_size > MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES:
            print(
              f"Payload exceeds {MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES} bytes (received {payload_size}).",
              file=sys.stderr,
            )
            sys.exit(1)

          payload = json.loads(raw_payload)
          if not isinstance(payload, dict):
            print("Payload must be a JSON object.", file=sys.stderr)
            sys.exit(1)

          payload = scrub(payload)
          workflow_live_write = os.environ.get("WORKFLOW_LIVE_WRITE", "false").lower() == "true"
          configured_secret = os.environ.get("LINEAR_BRIEF_WEBHOOK_SECRET", "")

          live_write = workflow_live_write
          if event_name == "repository_dispatch":
            payload_live_write = payload.get("live_write", False)
            if isinstance(payload_live_write, str):
              payload_live_write = payload_live_write.lower() == "true"
            live_write = bool(payload_live_write)

            provided_secret = payload.get("webhook_secret", "")
            if configured_secret:
              if not isinstance(provided_secret, str) or not hmac.compare_digest(provided_secret, configured_secret):
                print("repository_dispatch payload failed webhook secret validation.", file=sys.stderr)
                sys.exit(1)
            elif live_write:
              print(
                "Refusing live repository_dispatch write without LINEAR_BRIEF_WEBHOOK_SECRET configured.",
                file=sys.stderr,
              )
              sys.exit(1)

          payload.pop("webhook_secret", None)
          sanitized_payload = json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False)

          def sanitize_identifier(value: str) -> str:
            text = re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_-]", "", str(value))
            return text[:64] or "unknown"

          def sanitize_title(value: str) -> str:
            text = re.sub(r"[\r\n\t]+", " ", str(value)).strip()
            text = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", text)
            return text[:120] or "Untitled"

          linear_id = sanitize_identifier(payload.get("id", payload.get("identifier", "unknown")))
          title = sanitize_title(payload.get("title", "Untitled"))

          github_output = os.environ["GITHUB_OUTPUT"]
          with open(github_output, "a", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
            fh.write(f"live_write={'true' if live_write else 'false'}\n")
            # Use hash-based delimiter to avoid collision with payload content
            import hashlib
            delim = f"EOF_{hashlib.md5(sanitized_payload.encode()).hexdigest()}"
            fh.write(f"sanitized_payload<<{delim}\n")
            fh.write(sanitized_payload + "\n")
            fh.write(f"{delim}\n")
            # Escape title for safe shell output: percent-encode special characters
            title_escaped = title.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace('"', '\\"').replace("$", "\\$").replace("`", "\\`")
            fh.write(f"linear_id={linear_id}\n")
            fh.write(f"title_escaped={title_escaped}\n")
            fh.write(f"title={title}\n")

          print(f"Write mode: {'live' if live_write else 'dry-run'}")
          PY

      - name: Generate brief
        id: generate
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
          OPENAI_MODEL: gpt-5.4
          OPENAI_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS: "4096"
          LINEAR_PAYLOAD: ${{ steps.guardrails.outputs.sanitized_payload }}
        run: |
          ARGS=()
          if [ "${{ steps.guardrails.outputs.live_write }}" != "true" ]; then
            ARGS+=("--dry-run")
          fi
          python3 .github/scripts/linear_brief_generator.py "${ARGS[@]}"

          # Reuse sanitized metadata from guardrails.
          echo "linear_id=${{ steps.guardrails.outputs.linear_id }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "title=${{ steps.guardrails.outputs.title }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "date=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Validate generated content
        if: steps.guardrails.outputs.live_write == 'true'
        run: |
          git add "!/"
          python3 .github/scripts/validate_content.py --scope generated
          git reset HEAD

      - name: Create branch and PR
        if: steps.guardrails.outputs.live_write == 'true'
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          LINEAR_ID="${{ steps.generate.outputs.linear_id }}"
          TITLE="${{ steps.generate.outputs.title }}"
          DATE="${{ steps.generate.outputs.date }}"
          BRANCH="briefs/${LINEAR_ID}-${DATE}"

          git checkout -b "$BRANCH"
          git add "!/"

          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
            echo "No changes to commit."
            exit 0
          fi

          git commit -m "brief: ${TITLE} — ${DATE}"
          git push -u origin "$BRANCH"

          gh pr create \
            --title "Brief: ${TITLE}" \
            --body "$(cat <<EOF
          ## Research Brief

          Generated from Linear issue \`${LINEAR_ID}\`.

          **Review the brief in \`!/BRIEF-${LINEAR_ID}-${DATE}.md\`**, then:
          - Merge to land it in \`!/\` (the mailroom)
          - Move the file to the appropriate vault location after merge

          ---
          _Generated by Stage 1 Mesh pipeline_
          EOF
          )" \
            --base main \
            --head "$BRANCH"
